Football.title: Man Utd legend Gary Neville claims Tottenham getting RELEGATED would actually be ‘very good’ for Premier League
Football.content:
GARY NEVILLE has claimed Tottenham going down will BE GOOD for the Premier League.

Spurs sit one point above the relegation zone following their most recent league defeat to Crystal Palace, with an away trip to Liverpool coming up this weekend.





The north London side sacked Thomas Frank last month, hoping to stop the rot of a winless start to 2026 with the appointment of firefighter manager Igor Tudor.

However, as noted by SunSport’s Tom Barclay in the report of the club’s 5-2 defeat to Atletico Madrid in the Champions League on Tuesday, “arsonist” has been a more apt description.

Under Tudor Spurs remain winless, losing all four games the 47-year-old has been in charge, with 14 goals conceded and just five scored.

Spurs aren’t divorcing themselves from Tudor’s services just yet, but the rot cannot continue if the club are to avoid a previously unthinkable relegation.



The Europa League champions are one of the richest clubs in Europe and have a £1billion state-of-the-art stadium to boot.

However, Manchester United icon Neville believes Spurs going down won’t be the worst thing in the world for the league itself.

Speaking on the Stick to Football podcast, he said: “Seeing Tottenham go down wouldn’t be great for Tottenham fans and I think they’re an amazing club, but it would be very good for the Premier League.

“From a competitive perspective and the idea of Leeds staying up, of Sunderland staying up, cause I was more worried at the start of the season that every club that came up was going straight back down.



Cheltenham Festival: Get over £500 in free bets

“The league doesn’t necessarily need Spurs to go down, but needs clubs other than the ones who come up to go down.

“It would be sad to go down, but maybe they’ve had it coming.”

Spurs finished 17th under former manager Ange Postecoglou last season, who put that down to prioritising the Europa League, with the three newly-promoted clubs going straight back to the second tier.

But anyone who watched Spurs would have seen the issues ran far, far deeper than a simple change of priorities in competitions.

Injuries have certainly not helped, but unfathomable disciplinary decisions from senior players, including Cristian Romero, have seen them sent off at crucial moments.



A number of the new signings have also failed to make a positive mark.

Neville added that if Spurs do make another managerial change, they would need to do it sooner rather than later.

The 51-year-old said: “My view is they’ll make a change and stay up, just.

“I don’t think that group of players will buy into the idea of bringing someone in who’s not been a coach for a long time just because he used to play for the club.

“They’ll see right through that. It would need to be someone who has been coaching recently.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

“If it’s an ex-Tottenham man, sure, but Sean Dyche would take that job now, wouldn’t he? 100 per cent until the end of the season.

“They (Spurs fans) are worried now (about relegation). Those players don’t give two f****.”

Football.title: Wayne Rooney urges Tottenham to replace Igor Tudor with ROY KEANE to ‘go in and just absolutely batter them’
Football.content:
WAYNE ROONEY believes Tottenham need a new manager like fellow Manchester United legend Roy Keane to go in there and “batter” the players.

Spurs are deep in relegation trouble with only one point separating them from the danger zone and could be set to exit the Champions League after losing 5-2 at Atletico Madrid in their first leg of the Last 16 on Tuesday.





Interim manager Igor Tudor has now lost all four of his matches in charge of the North Londoners since his arrival last month when he replaced Thomas Frank and his position is already under fire.

Rooney, 40, watched the Premier League giants’ poor performances against Arsenal, Fulham, Crystal Palace as well as Atletico and is adamant Tudor should be sacked.

But Man Utd‘s record goalscorer strongly believes it’s not only the Croatian tactician’s fault as he also thinks the players have been an “absolute disgrace”.

So much so that the England legend insists Tottenham should hire a tough manager like Keane, 54, to put “some fear” into them.

Rooney told Stick to Football where he joined Keane, Gary Neville, Ian Wright and Jill Scott who all suggested Tudor should go: “Those players need to look at themselves, I think they have been an absolute disgrace, I do.

“The performances, the attitude, the lack of desire, the lack of fight, the lack of anything in them.

“I was watching the Palace game the other day and I really felt sorry for Tottenham fans, they are not even angry anymore they are disappointed and sad.

“Them players need to look at themselves, this is a disgrace.



CASINO SPECIAL – BEST CASINO BONUSES FROM £10 DEPOSITS

“I think they need someone to go in, like Roy, and batter them. They need some fear put into them.”

Keane made a name for himself as one of football’s most notorious hard men during a celebrated 17-year career.

The tough Irishman didn’t slow down when he crossed over to management as he quickly become known for his strict coaching with the likes of Ipswich and Sunderland.

Many fans and pundits have suggested Tottenham should sign a manager who knows the club, like Robbie Keane, Tim Sherwood, Harry Redknapp or Glenn Hoddle.

And Spurs great Gus Poyet told SunSport he would be very keen to take on the job.



This week’s Stick to Football podcast is available now on YouTube and all leading podcast platforms

Football.title: World Cup playoff chaos as Iraq face nightmare 12-hour drive through war-torn region to keep US dream alive
Football.content:
IRAQ could face a 12-hour bus journey through a war-affected region just to keep their World Cup dream alive.

The Middle Eastern nation are staring at the prospect of travelling by road to the Jordanian border if they are to fulfil their Intercontinental World Cup play-off in Mexico later this month.





The country has been badly affected by the ongoing conflict between Iran and the United States, with Iraqi airspace currently closed on safety grounds.

That has left the national team effectively stranded in Baghdad with limited options to leave the country.

Australian head coach Graham Arnold earlier this week called for Iraq’s play-off final against either Bolivia or Suriname to be postponed, warning that his squad were unable to travel.

But with FIFA yet to confirm any delay, Iraq’s football chiefs are scrambling to find a solution.



SunSport understands that discussions have taken place between the Iraqi FA and FIFA about potentially assembling a squad outside Iraq, using players already based abroad.

However, that proposal comes with major complications.

Several key players, along with members of the technical staff- are currently in Iraq and would still need to find a way to leave the country safely.

With time running out, another drastic solution has reportedly been suggested.



BEST FREE BETS AND BETTING SIGN UP OFFERS

SunSport understands Iraqi officials have discussed the possibility of travelling by road from Baghdad to neighbouring Jordan — a bus journey that could take up to 12 hour.

The route would allow the squad to cross the Jordanian border before catching an international flight to Mexico, where the Intercontinental play-off will be held.

However, the road journey would take place against the backdrop of the ongoing conflict in the region and is not considered entirely risk-free.



Sources say Iraq would ideally avoid making such a journey but may be forced into it if they want to keep their World Cup hopes alive.

SunSport has been told FIFA officials have even suggested Iraq leave Baghdad around March 20 and attempt the road trip to Jordan in order to meet travel deadlines.

Once in Jordan, the squad would be able to board a commercial flight and continue their journey to Mexico.

Meanwhile, the situation has become even more complicated following comments from Iran’s Sports Minister, who confirmed the country would not take part in the World Cup because of the war.

That development has sparked fresh debate within the region. Sources within the Iraqi FA say they would be willing to step in and replace Iran if FIFA allows changes to the tournament lineup.



Iraq’s argument would centre on the fact their own airspace closure has prevented normal travel arrangements.

Under one scenario being discussed, Iraq would go straight to the tournament to replace Iran while the United Arab Emirates, the country they defeated 3-2 to reach this stage, could instead be allowed to travel to Mexico for the play-off.

The UAE’s case is strengthened by the fact that some of their airports remain operational despite the wider regional disruption.

That would allow them to assemble their squad and fly directly to Mexico without needing the dangerous road journey currently facing Iraq.



SunSport has approached FIFA to ask whether they have received any official request from Iraq regarding replacing Iran in the competition.

FIFA were also asked whether the governing body had suggested that Iraq travel by road to Jordan in order to reach the decisive play-off match.

For now, Iraq’s players are left waiting for clarity as the clock ticks down.

Bolivia face Suriname in their Intercontinental play-off semi-final on March 26, with the winner scheduled to meet Iraq on March 31.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

The victor of that clash will claim the final ticket to join 47 other nations at the World Cup.



For Iraq, however, the biggest challenge may simply be getting to the match in the first place.

Football.title: Arsenal files: Lewis-Skelly frustrated, Gunners raid Man City for new signing, star’s younger brother joins training
Football.content:
MYLES LEWIS-SKELLY is suffering a disastrous season at Arsenal – and he has let his feelings known to manager Mikel Arteta.

The frustrated young left-back, once again an unused substitute in Wednesday’s 1-1 draw at Bayer Leverkusen, has not featured for the first team since a 4-0 FA Cup win over Wigan a month ago.





Lewis-Skelly enjoyed a brilliant season last term, making 26 starts and 39 appearances in total for the Gunners.

This season has been a different story.

He has made just 12 starts and 26 in total as he has fallen behind Piero Hincapie and Riccardo Calafiori in the queue for the left-back berth, even though he can play in midfield.

The 19-year-old has told Arteta of his major frustrations at a lack of action with the Arsenal manager telling him to be patient.



But his future next season is unclear and he could be sold or more likely loaned.

Before the Leverkusen game in Germany, Arteta was asked about Lewis-Skelly.

He said: “Every season is different. He’s had very good moments in the season, he’s had other moments when he hasn’t played that much.

“Obviously, a few days ago in the FA Cup (vs Mansfield) he was suspended and he could not play so there are various reasons.



“Sometimes, as well, the good form of your team-mates is another factor that contributes to that, but he’s doing well.”

Lewis-Skelly has six England caps although right now, he has no chance of going to the World Cup.

Comeback Kai

IT has been a week of comebacks for Kai Havertz.

The forward returned to Bayer Leverkusen on Wednesday night for the first time since leaving them for Chelsea six years ago as part of a £71million deal.

Havertz came through Leverkusen’s academy and is loved at the club, which presented him with a gift before kick-off against Arsenal.



CEO Fernando Carro, sporting director Simon Rolfes and Werner Wenning, chairman of the club’s shareholders committee handed Havertz a framed print pitch-side at the BayArena.

The quartet were all smiles as they did so, although Havertz was the only one smiling at the end after his late equaliser.

A visit to Leverkusen wasn’t the only comeback for the forward over the past week; he also returned to Mansfield for the second time in his career.

Havertz played at the League One side’s ground back in 2017 when Germany’s Under-19s faced England’s young stars.

It was a night to remember for a young Havertz, too, as he scored the third goal in a 3-1 win and was named man of the match.



Tout of order

ARSENAL are continuing their war against ticket touts.

Arsenal’s supporters had to pick up their Champions League tickets for Wednesday’s clash against Leverkusen in the German city while showing ID.

It was the second time this has happened and Arsenal are likely to do it for future away games this season – even in the Premier League – to make sure the tickets do not end up in the wrong hands.

The club are also still investing heavily to tackle touting for home games.



Since introducing digital ticketing and a new ballot system, they have cancelled nearly 74,000 accounts trying to obtain tickets while over 45,000 suspicious entries have been removed from ballots.

A big issue has been bots from overseas.

Arsenal have also banned over 17,500 memberships this season alone.

It is a war that Arsenal, along with other Premier League clubs may struggle to win, but they are still doing their best.



Just the ticket

ARSENAL fans have been left furious at ticket prices for the Gunners’ home Champions League last 16 leg against Bayer Leverkusen.

Supporters attending the Emirates on Tuesday night have had to pay between £78 and £145 for a general admission ticket.

Leverkusen, who are sixth in the Bundesliga, have been categorised as a Category A fixture by Arsenal – the most expensive ticket prices at the club.

A spokesperson for the Arsenal Supporters Trust said: “Leverkusen are opponents not really worthy of a steep Category A price.



“With a Wembley cup final and lots of cup games it’s a heavy time for fans’ wallets.

“Will Arsenal do the right thing and reward fans’ loyalty with a Category B price?”

Category B tickets are priced between £44.80 and £84.

From next season, Arsenal will be introducing two new ticket price categories: B+ and A+.

Champions League quarter-finals and semi-finals will be priced as Category A+ next season, meaning fans attending those matches will have to fork out between £90 and £168.



A taste for success

THE transfer window may be shut – but that has not stopped Arsenal raiding Manchester City for a new signing.

The Gunners have hired Pamela Ibarra as the new nutritionist for their men’s first team and she joins after a two-year stint at the Etihad.

Ibarra worked for City’s academy, first as an assistant nutritionist before being promoted to the role of academy performance nutritionist.

Prior to that, she spent time with governing body Ice Hockey UK and earlier in her career Ibarra worked as a coach for Nike.

She left City last month and announced her switch to Arsenal on LinkedIn this week.



Ibarra worte: “I’m thrilled to share that I’ve recently started a new role as Men’s First Team Nutritionist with Arsenal Football Club.

“Once again, timing couldn’t have been better. I’m very grateful to be given this opportunity and excited for the journey ahead alongside an exceptional team!

“Also, pretty happy I get to speak Spanish everyday. UP THE GUNNERS!”

Sharing is caring

PREMIER LEAGUE clubs often get a bit of flack for their jet-setting ways and for not being the most eco-friendly.



Indeed, just over 10 years ago, Arsenal were given plenty of stick for taking a 14-minute flight from Luton to Norwich for a game.

The Gunners were far greener in how they got back from Cologne on Wednesday night, though, after their draw with Bayer Leverkusen.

The plane that took them home was actually the one that had flown Aston Villa out to Lille earlier in the day for the first leg of their Europa League last-16 tie.

The jet continued from Lille to Cologne, picked up the Arsenal squad, and then landed back at Stansted in the early hours of Thursday morning.



Kell of a player

ARSENAL legend Kelly Smith had a huge 36-metre mural of herself unveiled in Watford last week.

The mural is at the Harlequin shopping centre in the town where Smith grew up before going on to score 130 goals in 156 appearances for Arsenal Women.

Smith, who is now an assistant coach for the women’s team, was blown away by the design – which was the work of the artist collective, MurWalls.

Famed artist, Mr Meana, led the project and this piece is the latest in his string of high-profile projects.

He has previously produced murals of Ed Sheeran and Sir Elton John, while ex Liverpool captain Steven Gerrard even commissioned him to create something for his son’s bedroom.



Nwan of our own

ETHAN NWANERI’S younger brother Emerson joined in with first-team training last week.

Emerson, 15, was called up to join the first-team squad in preparation for the Gunners’ FA Cup victory over Mansfield.

He was one of a number of academy players that were asked to join in on last Friday’s session.

Emerson is catching the eye in the Hale End setup and, in January, he became the youngest ever player to start for Arsenal’s Under-21s, playing in a Premier League 2 game against Liverpool to break the record previously set by his brother Ethan in 2022.



While last month, Emerson scored his first goal for Arsenal U18s – becoming the fifth youngest player ever to do so.

Ethan still holds the title of the youngest goalscorer after he netted when he was just 14 years, one month and 13 days old.

These records and performances have seen him pen scholarship terms with the Gunners for season.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

And, away from N5, Emerson was also part of the England U16s squad for last month’s games against Denmark, Spain and France.



It’s a stark difference to Ethan Nwaneri, who is being starved of minutes at Marseille following Roberto De Zerbi’s dismissal last month.

Football.title: Donald Trump warns Iran to STAY AWAY from World Cup ‘for their own life and safety’ in explosive statement
Football.content:
DONALD TRUMP has warned Iran’s football team to avoid this summer’s World Cup “for their own life and safety”.

Iran‘s national side qualified for the upcoming tournament in the US, Canada and Mexico after coming top of their group in Asian qualifying.





But huge doubts have emerged about their participation amid the ongoing conflict between the US and Iran.

Tensions have escalated after the US and Israel launched strikes on Iran, killing its supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

Iran’s sports minister has suggested the team should not play at the World Cup – something Trump agrees with.

Trump wrote on Truth Social: “The Iran National Soccer Team is welcome to The World Cup, but I really don’t believe it is appropriate that they be there, for their own life and safety.



“Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J. TRUMP.”

His stance has certainly changed from last week after he was asked about whether Iran should play at the World Cup.

Trump told Politico at the time: “I really don’t care.”

Iran’s Minister of Sports and Youth Ahmad Donyamali all but confirmed the team would not take their place at this summer’s competition.



He said this week: “Given that this corrupt government has assassinated our leader, under no circumstances do we have the appropriate conditions to participate in the World Cup.

“Our boys are not safe, and conditions for participation do not exist.”

BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

Donyamali added: “Over the past eight or nine months, two wars have been imposed on us and several thousand of our people have been killed and martyred.

“Therefore, we definitely do not have the possibility for participation.”



Since the first strikes by the US and Israel, Iran has retaliated with attacks on US bases in Bahrain, the United Arab Emirates, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Jordan, Qatar and Kuwait.

The World Cup is set to kick off on June 11 before ending on July 19.

Iran had been drawn in Group G alongside Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand.

Their first game was slated to against New Zealand on June 16, but organisers are now facing a dilemma over who to replace them with.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

One solution could be to allow Iraq to qualify directly given their struggles to fly out for their play-off game against the winners of Suriname and Bolivia.



That would leave Suriname and Bolivia to scrap it out for a place at the tournament.

Football.title: Spain vs Argentina Finalissima set to be moved to iconic 84,000-seater stadium days before match over security fears
Football.content:
SPAIN and Argentina may have to move their Finalissima clash to Real Madrid’s home after security fears exploded due to the war in Iran.

The Middle East has been rocked by retaliatory strikes following the assassination of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in joint US-Israeli airstrikes two weeks ago.





Qatar has been caught in the crossfire, with the Qatari Defense Ministry confirming they intercepted nine ballistic missiles and several drones just yesterday.

The Finalissima — pitting Euro 2024 winners Spain against Copa America champs Argentina — is currently scheduled for Doha’s Lusail Stadium on March 27.

According to AS, however, the ongoing tension in the region has raised security fears and thus the match could be moved to Real Madrid’s Santiago Bernabeu stadium.

Rome and Lisbon were also touted as back-up hosts, but Uefa bosses are said to be “very big” on using Real Madrid’s iconic ground.



However, the move faces a massive hurdle as Argentine football chiefs are reportedly “not in favour” of playing in the Spanish capital.

Journalist Gaston Edul claims the AFA is worried Lionel Messi’s side would essentially be playing a “home game” for Spain, handing their rivals a huge advantage.

However, football chiefs will continue to monitor the situation in the Middle East.

A recent Uefa statement read: “Uefa is closely monitoring the situation and is in touch with participating teams and local authorities where necessary.



BEST ONLINE CASINOS – TOP SITES IN THE UK

“All matches due to take place in Europe in the upcoming weeks are currently planned to be played as scheduled.

“With regards to the Finalissima scheduled for 27 March, Uefa is monitoring and carefully assessing all developments of the situation in cooperation with Conmebol and the LOC.”

Football.title: Chelsea star Pedro Neto facing Champions League ban after shoving ballboy and sparking mass brawl in PSG clash
Football.content:
PEDRO NETO is facing a Champions League ban after shoving a Paris Saint-Germain ball boy on Wednesday night.

The Chelsea winger, 26, sparked a mass brawl in injury time at Parc des Princes after pushing the youngster into the advertising boards.





Neto quickly apologised and gave his shirt to the boy with Portugal team-mate Vitinha coming to his aid to help with the translation.

But Uefa’s disciplinary body opened disciplinary proceedings against the winger, which is likely to lead to a ban.



In a statement, Uefa said: “Disciplinary proceedings have been instigated against Chelsea player Pedro Lomba Neto for unsporting behaviour in accordance with Article 15(1)(a)(v) of Uefa Disciplinary Regulations.

“Uefa’s disciplinary bodies will take a decision on this case in due course.”

Uefa’s guidlines do not include a standard suspension for unsporting conduct but Neto is expected to be handed a one-match ban if found guilty, meaning he would miss next week’s second-leg against PSG.

The 5-2 first-leg defeat has already left Enzo Maresca’s side with a mountain to climb to reach the quarter-finals.



Blues great Eden Hazard was given a three-match ban by The FA for kicking a 17-year-old Swansea ballboy in the 2013 Carabao Cup semi-final.

Any ban for Neto would only apply to European football.

Neto told TNT Sports: “I want to apologise to the ball boy. I have already spoken to him.

“I’m not like this. It was in the heat of the moment and I want to apologise. I gave him my shirt.

“He could see what had happened and was happy with the situation.”

Football.title: ‘Wasn’t a pretty sight’ – Jamie O’Hara issues statement after missing prime talkSPORT show through illness
Football.content:
JAMIE O’HARA missed his golden chance to lay into talkSPORT co-host Jason Cundy after being struck down by FOOD POISONING.

The former Tottenham midfielder has been taking pelters from ex-Blue Cundy for weeks as his side plummet towards the relegation zone.





And Chelsea’s 5-2 defeat to PSG on Wednesday was the ideal time for O’Hara, 39, to have his revenge.

But the The Sports Bar presenter wasn’t available to take digs at Cundy or Liam Rosenior‘s side.

Instead, he had to abandon ship pre-show after falling ill having gone in to work.



Pal Cundy was drafted in at short notice to replace him after covering the game remotely for Chelsea.

Writing on X at 10.17pm, O’Hara said: “The one time I get to go at Cundy I’ve got food poisoning, sorry guys I can’t be on tonight I tried getting through it but it wasn’t a pretty sight for the people at Talksport towers, hopefully back in tomorrow, Up the Chels!!””

Cundy, who had been doing commentary at Stamford Bridge during the game, rushed over to the studio to save the day.

He has been mocking O’Hara with his viral Ange Postecoglou mask and clown outfit.



After being battered by Crystal Palace at home last week, O’Hara was left fuming at Cundy’s antics.

O’Hara shouted and yelled “SHUT UP” after Cundy introduced the show with his typical ‘Has anyone seen’ segment.

And things escalated so much that O’Hara walked out midway through the broadcast.

Chelsea going from 2-2 away in Paris to being crushed 5-2, the same scoreline Spurs were beaten by Atletico Madrid the night before, offered O’Hara a much-needed opportunity to laugh at his mate.

Illness made that impossible with Adrian Durham announcing the late in change in line-up, saying: “By the way, Jamie O’Hara, sick of Spurs.



“So sick he’s off tonight, off The Sports Bar.

“The good news is it’s Jason Cundy and Carlton Cole coming up very soon, both of them used to play for Chelsea so laugh it up, enjoy it all. Has anyone seen, you make it all up.”

When Cundy did rock up, he was told: “He’s sweating, he’s looking a bit like O’Hara just before he left us.”

And the rattled pundit added: “Oh my gosh. Do you know, I thought it was a joke.

“I’ve come off air at Stamford Bridge during the game and I’ve seen missed calls from Ryan saying O’Hara is ill.



“He said, ‘Can you come in’ and you just want to poke fun at me.

“OK, what’s the story? He’s not ill is he?

“Well he is, he’s sent me a photograph, I’ve messaged him.”

Cundy added: “How have we lost that game?

“I’m sick, I’m sick.”

He was then told, “Sick with failure!”

Responding to a video of Cundy’s arrival on X, talkSPORT part-timer Laura Woods joked about the Ange mask, writing: “It’s so strange seeing Jason’s actual face now”.

Football.title: Ex-Chelsea star reveals health scare that left him paralysed during European final
Football.content:
Ex-Chelsea star reveals health scare that left him paralysed during European final

Michael Duberry has opened up on the scary experience of playing for Chelsea in the 1998 Cup Winners’ Cup final against Stuttgart while suffering from Bell’s Palsy

View 3 Images Michael Duberry spent six years at Chelsea (Image: Paul Mcfegan/Sportsphoto/Allstar via Getty Images)

Michael Duberry has revealed that he played for Chelsea in the 1998 Cup Winners’ Cup final while suffering from a medical condition. Duberry spent six years playing for the Blues and the 1997/98 season was the height of his success with the club.

‌



The centre-back was part of the side which beat Stuttgart 1-0 to win the Cup Winners' Cup on May 13, 1998. Chelsea's team that day in Stockholm contained the likes of Frank Leboeuf, Gianfranco Zola, Dennis Wife, Gus Poyet, Roberto Di Matteo and Gianluca Vialli.

‌



Duberry started in central defence and played the full 90 minutes, despite a fraught build-up to the final. He was diagnosed with Bell's Palsy two weeks before the match - a condition which causes sudden weakness or paralysis in the facial muscles.

‌



"The one that sticks out, for me because it is a foundation of my whole resilience and who I was, [is the Cup Winners’ Cup]. I suffered, not many people know this, I don’t tell the story," he said on the SACKED! podcast.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

UEFA release statement on Pedro Neto punishment after Chelsea star pushes ballboy READ MORE :

“It wasn’t like it is now where everything is news but the biggest game of my life at the time, you mentioned the Cup Winners’ Cup. I was 20, 21, and two weeks before I suffered from Bell’s Palsy.

‌



"People might be going: ‘What is Bell’s Palsy?' In essence, it’s a mini stroke, down the side of your face. It just collapses. Someone says: ‘Look at your face.’ And I go: ‘Yeah, look at your face.’ Having a bit of banter.

"And they say: ‘No, seriously Dubes, go and look at your face.’ I go to the toilet and I look at my face and I can’t move my face. I look like Sloth in the Goonies if you picture a Black version of that.

View 3 Images Chelsea won the Cup Winners' Cup final in 1998 (Image: Andrew Stenning/Mirrorpix/Getty Images)

‌



"I’m going ‘Oh my God.’ I phone up my physio to see our club doctor at Chelsea, get in the car, and all the lads are having a bit of banter on the way out. Going, driving down on the journey, and I’m looking in the mirror at my face, I can’t move my face.

"I see the doctor and he says I’ve got Bell’s Palsy. What is Bell’s Palsy? I don’t know what Bell’s Palsy is? Well, how long do you have it for, because I’m thinking we’ve got a cup final. We just beat Middlesbrough in the League Cup. It can be two weeks, two months, two years."

"In my head I’m thinking: ‘Don’t let this disturb you.’ I was always the young one thinking: ‘I mustn’t be the weak link.’ I’ve got internationals around me and I think ‘This can’t affect me.’

‌



View 3 Images Gianfranco Zola won the game for Chelsea (Image: Dave Shopland/Chelsea FC via Getty Images)

"Luckily, the magic man Gianfranco Zola scored the winning goal and we won. I always remember doing the celebrations, I always use that picture when I do some of my talks," he added.

“And I say to people: ‘What do you see in that picture?’ And they say: ‘Celebrations, glory.’ And I say: ‘Resilience.’ Because in pictures, if you see my face there, anyone who has had it can straight away, and it just says ‘resilience’ because I had the Bell’s Palsy.

‌



"That’s one of my biggest accomplishments, hurdles, things I’ve ever overcome in my life. Having Bell’s Palsy and playing a major cup final and winning is the biggest thing for me."

Football.title: UEFA release statement on Pedro Neto punishment after Chelsea star pushes ballboy
Football.content:
UEFA release statement on Pedro Neto punishment after Chelsea star pushes ballboy

Chelsea forward Pedro Neto faces UEFA investigation after shoving a ball boy to the ground in the final stages of their 5-2 Champions League defeat to Paris Saint-Germain

View 2 Images Pedro Neto pushed the ballboy over in the latter stages of Chelsea's defeat at Paris Saint-Germain (Image: PA)

UEFA have launched disciplinary proceedings against Chelsea forward Pedro Neto following an incident involving a ball boy during Wednesday night's 5-2 Champions League loss to Paris Saint-Germain. Neto pushed the ball boy to the ground in the closing stages of the match, which ignited tensions among both sets of players.

‌



The winger rushed to collect the ball and the ball boy hesitated to hand it over, prompting Neto to react. Following a scuffle between two players, Neto emerged and apologised. Now, UEFA have confirmed they will examine the incident.

‌



"Disciplinary proceedings have been opened against Chelsea player Pedro Lomba Neto for unsporting conduct, in accordance with Article 15(1)(a)(v) of the UEFA Disciplinary Regulations," they said. "UEFA's disciplinary bodies will make a decision on this case in due course."

‌



Pedro Neto issues apology after Chelsea star PUSHES PSG ball boy to floor READ MORE :

Chelsea star stops Filip Jorgensen going straight down tunnel after Reece James incident READ MORE :

Following the defeat in Paris, Neto emerged and held his hands up after the controversial moment. After escaping a booking, VAR chose against showing a red card.

FOLLOW OUR CHELSEA FB PAGE! Latest Blues news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Speaking to TNT Sports after the match, Neto said: "I want to apologise to the ball boy. I have already spoken with him. With the emotions of the game, we were losing and I wanted to pick up the ball.

‌



"He had the ball with him and I gave him a little push. I saw that I hurt him in the heat of the moment and I went there to apologise. I gave him my shirt as well. That cannot happen and I'm really, really sorry about that. I just want to say one more time that I'm really sorry to him.

View 2 Images Chelsea forward Pedro Neto appeared to push a PSG ball-boy as he attempted to retrieve the ball (Image: beIN Sports)

"I feel that I have to apologise to him. It was in the heat of the moment, you know how football is, you have a lot of emotions. I wanted to get the ball fast, I gave him a push and I want to say sorry to him.

‌



"He came to speak to me at the end and even laughed as he was happy that I gave him the shirt. I said sorry like 35 times. I'm really sorry about that and the situation.

"He knew what happened as well so he is happy with the situation. Vitinha said to him too that I'm not like that. I was worried about him and I'm really sorry about the situation."

Football.title: Ex-Chelsea boss Graham Potter lands new deal after two games - despite losing them both
Football.content:
Ex-Chelsea boss Graham Potter lands new deal after two games - despite losing them both

Graham Potter has seen his Sweden contract extended despite him losing both of the games he'd led with the nation still harbouring hopes of a World Cup appearance

View 2 Images Graham Potter has signed a deal to remain as Sweden boss until 2030

Graham Potter has been handed a new four-year deal as Sweden boss - despite losing the two games he's had since taking charge.

‌



The ex-Chelsea boss found his way back into management by venturing into the international game last year. Potter's stock in Sweden remains high given his successful spell at Ostersund - taking them from the country's fourth division to the top flight, while also winning a domestic cup in 2017.

‌



Potter had an underwhelming stint at West Ham but landed the Sweden job shortly after and has now agreed to extend the arrangement into a long-term one. The Scandinavian nation face Ukraine in a one-match play-off in two weeks' time, before the winner faces Poland or Albania for a place at this summer's World Cup.

‌



Despite a poor qualifying campaign they were given the chance to make this summer's major tournament due to their Nations League results. Swedish FA's secretary general Niclas Carlnen was thrilled that Potter had committed himself to the role - seeing him as their long-term boss.

He said: "I am incredibly happy about the continued collaboration with Graham Potter, who is a solid person whose leadership I appreciate and have strong faith in.

Simon Jordan brutally slams Arsenal icon Martin Keown for Gunners 'bias' READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



"The long-term perspective gives players and managers peace of mind and the opportunity to create continuity in their activities with a view to future championships. Our national teams are of great importance to Swedish football and to many people around the country. Now we look forward to the coming years, but first and foremost, hopefully a World Cup summer."

Sweden do boast the likes of Alexander Isak, Viktor Gyokeres and Dejan Kulusevski when fit - giving them a formidable forward line. They didn't qualify for the last World Cup in 2022, but four years earlier had made the last eight, where they were beaten by England.

View 2 Images Graham Potter landed the Sweden job off the back of a poor stint as West Ham boss (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

‌



"To be able to continue in this role means a lot to me," Potter said on his new deal. "I feel both a great pride and a great responsibility. It's a big day for me and a fantastic opportunity to do something important going forward.

"Sweden is a football country with a proud history of teams that have made it to the finals of major championships and competed there, which we want to return to."

Football.title: Gary Neville delivers brutal Tottenham relegation statement -  'very good for Premier League'
Football.content:
Gary Neville delivers brutal Tottenham relegation statement - 'very good for Premier League'

Gary Neville has had his say on Tottenham Hotspur's relegation battle this season as they face a stunning drop into the Championship under Igor Tudor

View 2 Images Gary Neville has had his say on Tottenham's relegation battle (Image: The Overlap )

Gary Neville has suggested that Tottenham Hotspur's potential relegation could be "very good for the Premier League". Spurs are teetering just a single point above the relegation zone with nine league matches left to play.

‌



The North London club's performance has taken a nosedive under temporary head coach Igor Tudor, who has suffered defeats in his first four games at the helm. The pressure on the ex-Juventus and Lazio boss ramped up following Tuesday's 5-2 drubbing by Atletico Madrid in the Champions League.

‌



Tudor is now preparing his team for a trip to Anfield to face Liverpool on Sunday, with Tottenham potentially slipping into the bottom three if West Ham and Nottingham Forest secure victories in their preceding fixtures.

‌



Wayne Rooney and Gary Neville in disagreement over Michael Carrick amid Man Utd dilemma READ MORE :

Ex-Manchester United defender turned pundit, Neville, shared his thoughts on The Overlap podcast, suggesting that Spurs' potential drop could benefit the competitiveness of England's top-tier football league.

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

He said: "I refuse to predict relegation. I hated it, the idea of seeing a club go down.

‌



"But I must admit seeing Tottenham go down wouldn't be great for Tottenham fans and I think Tottenham are an amazing club, but it would be very good for the Premier League from a competitive perspective and the idea of Leeds staying up, of Sunderland staying up.

View 2 Images Relegation-threatened Tottenham are in turmoil (Image: Julian Finney, Getty Images)

"I'll tell you what I was more worried about at the start of the season, that every club which came up was just going straight back down again.

‌



"I think the Premier League doesn't necessarily need Tottenham to go down, but it needs clubs other than the ones that come up to go down.

Find out more here. Bet 18+ only. Terms and conditions apply. See paper for details.

"It would be sad to go down, but maybe they've had it coming."

‌



Despite expressing his thoughts on Spurs' possible relegation to the Championship - the 51 year old doesn't foresee such a scenario unfolding for the North London club.

He added: "I don't think they will go down.

"If you're going to make another change, that for me will probably need to be today or tomorrow because you don't wait."

Football.title: Donald Trump tells Iran they should NOT attend World Cup for 'own life and safety'
Football.content:
Donald Trump tells Iran they should NOT attend World Cup for 'own life and safety'

The World Cup kicks off in North America in the summer and United States President Donald Trump has issued a sinister warning.

View 2 Images President Donald Trump has issued a warning to Iran ahead of the World Cup as the ongoing conflict in the Middle East continues. (Image: AP)

Donald Trump says Iran should stay away from this summer's World Cup for 'their own life and safety' amid the ongoing conflict in the Middle East. There are less than 100 days until the prestigious international tournament kicks off but the scrutiny surrounding the competition continues to intensify.

‌



Held across the United States, Canada and Mexico from June 11, Iran are one of the nations who have qualified for the tournament. All of their games were due to be held in American cities as they took on New Zealand, Belgium and Egypt in Los Angeles and Seattle.

‌



However, with tensions rising in the Middle East as the Iran War heads towards the two-week mark, the ramifications on the sporting world are now coming to the fore.

‌



In a post to social media platform, Truth Social, he said: "The Iran National Soccer Team is welcome to The World Cup, but I really don’t believe it is appropriate that they be there, for their own life and safety.

"Thank you for your attention to this matter! President DONALD J. TRUMP."

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



It comes a day after Iranian Minister of Sports and Youth, Ahmad Donyamali, said that the nation was not in a position to participate in this summer's tournament.

The war has seen Iranian Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei killed following strikes on the region. The conflict has escalated after the US, and Israeli forces, continued their attacks on Iran, who have since retaliated with missiles directed towards neighbouring Gulf nations.

On Monday, the US-based Human Rights Activists in Iran (HRANA) reported that 1,761 people had been killed, including 1,245 civilians, 194 of them children since the war started on February 28.

‌



Donyamali told state television: "Our children are not safe and, fundamentally, such conditions for participation do not exist.

"Given the malicious actions ⁠they have carried out against Iran, ⁠they have forced two wars on us over eight or nine months and have killed and martyred thousands of our people. Therefore, we ⁠certainly cannot have such a presence.

"Our children are not safe and, fundamentally, such conditions for participation do not exist. Given the malicious actions they have carried out against Iran, they have forced two wars on us over eight or nine months and have killed and martyred thousands of our people. Therefore, we certainly cannot have such a presence."

‌



View 2 Images Donald Trump (left) alongside FIFA president Gianni Infantino. (Image: PA)

The revelation from Trump comes a day after FIFA president Gianni Infantino was said to have been told by the US President that the Iranian football team were welcome to play in the tournament.

Infantino said: "This evening, I met with the President of the United States, Donald J. Trump to discuss the status of preparations for the upcoming World Cup, and the growing excitement as we are set to kick off in just 93 days.

Article continues below

"We also spoke about the current situation in Iran, and the fact that the Iranian team has qualified to participate in the World Cup. During the discussions, President Trump reiterated that the Iranian team is, of course, welcome to compete in the tournament in the United States.

"We all need an event like the World Cup to bring people together now more than ever, and I sincerely thank the President of the United States for his support, as it shows once again that football unites the world."

Football.title: Tottenham icon opens door to shock return - 'Who wouldn't want the job'
Football.content:
Tottenham icon opens door to shock return - 'Who wouldn't want the job'

Tottenham are falling down the Premier League table, winless in 11, but former player Jurgen Klinsmann still sees it as an attraction job amid talk of another managerial change

View 2 Images Jurgen Klinsmann still sees the Tottenham job as an attractive proposition

Jurgen Klinsmann has opened the door to a Tottenham return as he talked up the manager's job and believes the person in charge needs an emotional connection to the club.

‌



The German played 68 matches for Spurs, scoring 38 goals in his two spells in north London and remains well-loved by the fanbase. Morale at Tottenham is at an all-time low with the club facing the genuine possibility of relegation to the Championship.

‌



They are already on their second manager of the season after Igor Tudor replaced Thomas Frank, but after only four games at the helm questions are being asked of the Croatian, who is yet to avoid defeat. A third man could yet grace the dugout this season - and Klinsmann still sees it as an attractive job.

‌



Tottenham are one point above the bottom three with fan discontent at an all-time high. Supporters walked out at half-time in a recent home game and Klinsmann insists they need to find more fight if they want to remain in the Premier League.

He said on ESPN: "Who wouldn't want the job, it is Tottenham. Whoever you choose, you need a person who can connect to everyone emotionally, that knows the club, that feels the club, that feels the people. Because, to get out of this mess, they need to develop a fighting spirit, a really nasty, ugly, fighting spirit and that goes only over the emotions.

Thibaut Courtois reveals message he sent to Antonin Kinsky after Tottenham horror show READ MORE :

JOIN US ON FACEBOOK! Latest news, analysis and much more on Mirror Football's Facebook page

‌



"So you don't need to have to bring in the mastermind of tactical stuff or whatever, you need to have somebody who gets everybody onboard and go and get these games done in a positive way and get everybody behind the fact that they are in danger of going down to the Championship."

Tottenham have been Premier League ever presents, but that record is under serious threat. Their next game sees them face Liverpool - defeat would extend their winless run to 12 games. After that they will face a titanic clash with Nottingham Forest, which could have a huge bearing on who goes down.

View 2 Images Jurgen Klinsmann played at Tottenham in the 1990s

‌



A number of previous Tottenham figures have been linked with a possible return, should the hierarchy chose to sack Tudor. Harry Redknapp has ruled himself out whilst there has been talk of ex-captain Tim Sherwood also making a return.

Robbie Keane is currently plying his trade at Ferencvaros in Hungary - he's another man to be linked with the Tottenham gig. Klinsmann has inadvertently put his name into the mix with his comments. He hasn't held a job since 2004, when his 18-game stint in charge of South Korea came to an end.

Football.title: Jurgen Klopp taking big management job 'discussed' by colleagues as Red Bull 'not happy'
Football.content:
Jurgen Klopp taking big management job 'discussed' by colleagues as Red Bull 'not happy'

Former Liverpool boss Jurgen Klopp's future continues to be a topic of speculation while in his current job with Red Bull, and many football fans are waiting for him to return to management

View 2 Images Jurgen Klopp has been linked with a return to football management (Image: RONNY HARTMANN, AFP via Getty Images)

Jurgen Klopp’s future with Red Bull is reportedly being discussed amid rumours of a potential return to management. The former Liverpool boss has been Red Bull’s global head of soccer since January last year, having left Anfield at the end of the 2023/24 season.

‌



Klopp helps oversee Red Bull’s entire football network, which includes RB Leipzig in Germany, Red Bull Salzburg in Austria and the New York Red Bulls in the United States, along with several others. Ever since leaving Liverpool, there have been plenty of rumours that Klopp could return to management, either with a club or an international team, particularly the Germany national side.

‌



Now, there has been a fresh update on Klopp’s future, this time from Red Bull’s perspective. A report in German news outlet Sport Bild has claimed there have been discussions held about Klopp’s future with the group.

‌



Alan Brazil misses Cheltenham talkSPORT show again as stand-in issues health update READ MORE :

The report claims that the talks have been centred around the possibility of Klopp taking the Germany national team in the near future.

It has been stated, though, that Klopp's return to club management is not expected to happen despite the ongoing speculation.

Bild also claim that Red Bull consider it an open secret that the group are not entirely happy with Klopp’s current work.

‌



FOLLOW OUR LIVERPOOL FB PAGE! Latest Reds news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Klopp has been heavily linked with a return to club management for some time, with rumours that a move could happen to the likes of Real Madrid, who face long-term uncertainty in the dugout.

However, Klopp’s agent, Marc Kosicke, recently made it clear that the former Liverpool boss is expected to remain at Red Bull.

‌



"Jurgen Klopp is very happy with his current role at Red Bull, and what is being said about negotiations to coach Real Madrid are just rumours for now," Kosicke told 365Scores in a statement last week.

The rumours that Klopp could become Germany boss in the future have not gone away, either, with suggestions that the former Liverpool boss could replace Julian Nagelsmann either side of this summer’s World Cup.

"There's no need to answer questions about things that are just rumours," Kosicke said. "Nobody has contacted us at this time."

‌



View 2 Images Former Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has been in his role at Red Bull since January 2025 (Image: Getty Images)

Klopp has already made his feelings about a potential return to club management clear, and why he is content to remain in his current role with Red Bull.

“I’m in a place, as a person, where I’m completely at peace with where I am. I don’t want to be somewhere else,” Klopp told AFP in January. “I don’t get up and excited if Real Madrid are showing interest. If they would be, but it’s the media.

Article continues below

“Do I want to coach again? At the moment, I would say no, but I cannot say never, never, never. I don’t expect to change my mind, but I don’t know.”

Football.title: Wayne Rooney and Gary Neville in disagreement over Michael Carrick amid Man Utd dilemma
Football.content:
Wayne Rooney and Gary Neville in disagreement over Michael Carrick amid Man Utd dilemma

Manchester United are edging closer to decision time regarding their next permanent manager, with Michael Carrick currently in charge until the end of this season

View 2 Images Wayne Rooney has thrown his support behind Michael Carrick (Image: The Overlap)

Wayne Rooney believes that Michael Carrick will deserve the Manchester United job if they qualify for the Champions League. But Gary Neville insists that Old Trafford chiefs need to go for 'best in class manager available'.

‌



Carrick, 44, has guided United to six wins, one draw and one defeat after eight games in the hot seat. The ex-Red Devils midfielder was put in charge until the end of this season, but a drastic upturn in results since the days of Ruben Amorim have seen him installed as favourite for the permanent gig.

‌



United are third in the Premier League table, putting them on course to qualify for the Champions League, with five spots expected to be available. Rooney has predicted that Carrick's side will qualify and says that doing so 'should' earn him the role.

‌



"I think if they get Champions League football, he should get the job," Rooney declared on the Stick to Football podcast. "He puts himself right up there if they're looking for new managers. But he definitely puts himself in pole position, I think."

FOLLOW OUR MAN UTD FB PAGE! Latest United news and more on our dedicated Facebook page

Luis Enrique disagreement emerges as Man Utd left with tough Michael Carrick decision READ MORE :

Neville has been markedly more non-committal on Carrick's future, warning United not to go down the same route as what led them to appoint Ole Gunnar Solskjaer after a successful interim period in 2019. Speaking last month, the former Red Devils right-back told Sky Sports: "Manchester United have to pursue another manager right now around the fact of you don't know how the results are going to go.

‌



"But little things like Thomas Tuchel re-signing with England does start to take away the options for Manchester United and they don't harm Michael's chances of getting the job. If he gets United into the Champions League there will be a lot of fever around that he should be appointed.

"I'm not against him being appointed, I love him to bits. But I think Manchester United should go for the best class of manager available.

Should Michael Carrick get the Manchester United job permanently? Have your say in the comments section.

‌



View 2 Images Michael Carrick has changed Manchester United's fortunes for the better (Image: Robbie Jay Barratt - AMA/Getty Images)

"They've had young and inexperienced managers in the last two picks. Ole Gunnar Solskjaer has come in before as an ex-player and that didn't work in the end. So I feel removing as much risk as possible is the right option."

Carrick hopes to strengthen his case on Sunday when United welcome Aston Villa to Old Trafford. Villa are only below them on goal difference, setting up a mouth-watering clash.

Football.title: Humphries, MVG star at European Darts Trophy but Littler absent in Germany again
Football.content:
Luke Humphries and Michael van Gerwen are among the star names who will compete at the 2026 Elten Safety Shoes European Darts Trophy, beginning on Friday.

The tournament, held in Goettingen, Germany, will also welcome star names including Gerwyn Price and Gian van Veen.

But Luke Littler, 19, who won the UK Open at the weekend for the second consecutive year, will be absent along with Nathan Aspinall, Gary Anderson and James Wade.

'The Nuke' has previously received a hostile reception from crowds in Germany, and withdrew from last year's European Darts Grand Prix, held in Sindelfingen.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Luke Littler gestures to the Berlin crowd as jeers ring out after a visit of 26 in his match with Chris Dobey in last year's Premier League Darts

In the weeks before, the world champion was booed on a Premier League night in Berlin and then booed again at the German Darts Grand Prix, in Munich.

Taking to X after the latter tournament, he said: "Shouldn't have been in Munich but had to play anyway. Next one in Germany for me is Dortmund and I am glad to say that."

The winner of the European Darts Trophy, who will compete in a final scheduled for Sunday, will scoop a £35,000 prize.

Football.title: Coles, Cox land bumper paydays in Men's Hundred auction
Football.content:
James Coles fetched the highest ​price in the first round of bidding in the Men's Hundred auction as London Spirit paid £390,000 for the 21-year-old Sussex all-rounder.

Welsh Fire secured England's Joe Root and last year's tournament MVP Jordan Cox as they spent over half their budget on the duo.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Welsh Fire captain Phil Salt was pleased with the prices that his side paid for Jordan Cox and Joe Root during the Hundred auction

All eight franchises entered the bidding hall with just over £1m at their disposal to build their squads, with each already locking in four players apiece as direct signings and the Cardiff-based outfit spent big in the opening minutes.

They picked up Root - England's record Test run-scorer - for £240,000 and gambled even bigger (£300,000) on last year's leading scorer Cox.

After finishing bottom last year, the Fire's new investors fronted by Washington Freedom owner Sanjay Govil, splashed the cash in a bid to attract big name talent but left themselves just £487,000 to spend on their remaining eight places.

England's leading white-ball spinner Adil Rashid collected £250,000 from Southern Brave, James Vince switched to MI London for £190,000 and Jonny Bairstow fetched £160,000 as London Spirit's first new arrival.

Sunrisers Leeds, the rebranded successors to Northern Superchargers, were among those not to get a deal over the line in the first set of marquee players - surprisingly allowing all three hometown favourites Root, Rashid and Bairstow to go elsewhere.

Pressure on 'promising' Coles to perform

Ravi Bopara, speaking at The Hundred Auction:

"As a young man with that price tag on his head, there will naturally be some pressure on James Coles to perform. I guess London Spirit see something in him.

"I've had the pleasure of playing with him at Sussex - he's a very promising cricketer. He has plenty of time when he bats, and he contributes with the ball as well.

"He's done well in franchise cricket around the world, so it's a great opportunity for him. Hopefully he's young - and naïve enough - not to feel the pressure of the price tag."

"We've had the highest bid for young James Coles - how magnificent was that?

"His ceiling is huge. He has foundations in the shortest format of the game, but he also has a strong base with eight first‑class hundreds.

"He impressed with the England Lions, he can bat in the top five, and he offers a huge amount with the ball in the powerplay or the middle overs.

"All-rounders are the most sought‑after players, so no surprise with Coles at all."

Adil Rashid - Southern Brave - £250,000

Joe Root - Welsh Fire - £240,000

Dan Lawrence - Sunrisers Leeds - £210,000

James Vince - MI London - £190,000

Jonny Bairstow - London Spirit - £160,000

Pakistan represented as Tariq joins Phoenix

Image: Pakistan's Usman Tariq was signed by Birmingham Phoenix at the inaugural men's auction

Pakistan will be represented at this summer's Hundred tournament after spinner Usman Tariq was signed by Birmingham Phoenix at the inaugural men's auction.

The England and Wales Cricket Board was compelled to issue formal denials of an effective 'shadow ban' being employed by some of the sides who have taken Indian investment, releasing a joint statement from all eight franchises affirming players would not be excluded on nationality.

The country's only female representatives - Fatima Sana and Sadia Iqbal - went unsold in the women's auction, Shaheen Shah Afridi withdrew from the men's sale on Wednesday evening and fellow quick Haris Rauf failed to attract an offer when he was put up for his reserve price of £100,000.

However fears of Pakistani players being frozen out proved unfounded as spinner Abrar Ahmed was signed by Indian-owned Sunrisers Leeds and Usman Tariq joined Birmingham Phoenix.

Tariq guaranteed Pakistani representation in this summer's tournament when he was signed for £140,000 by the Edgbaston-based Phoenix, though it was notable that only they and another American-backed side, Trent Rockets, made offers.

It was, therefore, a significant moment when Sunrisers - owned in full by the group behind Indian Premier League outfit Sunrisers Hyderabad - went in for, and won, the highly-rated leg-spinner Abrar at a cost of £190,000. Chief executive Kavya Maran was there in person to raise the paddle.

When is the 2026 edition of The Hundred?

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of Oval Invincibles' 26-run win over Trent Rockets in the men's Hundred final as Sam Billings' side completed a historic threepeat

The tournament runs from Tuesday July 21 to Sunday August 16, with Sunrisers Leeds Women and MI London Men starting their title defences on day one, at The Kia Oval.

Every match from the men's and women's competitions will be live on Sky Sports, including the eliminators on Friday August 14 and finals two days later, with every day seeing a women's game precede a men's fixture at the same venue.

How does the tournament work?

Teams play eight group games, meeting six sides once and their local rivals twice.

MI London face London Spirit on two occasions, with the other home and away fixtures Sunrisers Leeds vs Manchester Super Giants, Trent Rockets vs Birmingham Phoenix, and Southern Brave vs Welsh Fire.

The men's and women's sides that top the league phase will qualify directly for the finals, with the sides ending second and third meeting in the eliminator to decide the table-toppers' opponents.

Football.title: Fifth time lucky for Home By The Lee in shock Stayers' Hurdle success
Football.content:
Home By The Lee made it fifth time lucky as Joseph O’Brien’s star finally won the Stayers' Hurdle at the Cheltenham Festival.

Partnered by JJ Slevin, the 11-year-old was always prominent and kept finding as he went on to score by a length and a quarter from Ballyburn.

Dan Skelton's Kabral Du Mathan had travelled powerfully, but stamina doubts proved true as he struggled up the Cheltenham hill.

Last year's winner Bob Olinger stayed on to take third.

O'Brien said: "We were just hoping for a good run. It was a fantastic ride by JJ and I'm so delighted for Sean O'Driscoll and his family as they bred him and he's an 11-year-old and they've been with him all the way. He's as tough as nails. It was a huge team effort by everyone at home.

Image: Home By The Lee (red) fights past Kabral Du Mathan in the Stayers' Hurdle

"We've raced him a bit more often than we usually do. We thought we'd get him running but we normally keep him very fresh.

"He's all stamina and JJ gave him a fantastic ride. He made all the right moves at every stage. Of all the horses I've ever trained he's the winningmost in number of wins.

"We've had him since maybe he just turned four, so he's been there a long time. He trains every day with enthusiasm and when he's off he gets bored. He loves his work, loves his job, loves his racing and has never had a lame step in his life. He's a superstar."

Heart Wood too good for Ryanair rivals

Heart Wood eased to victory to take the Ryanair Chase in convincing style at the Cheltenham Festival.

Trained by Henry de Bromhead and ridden by Darragh O'Keeffe, Heart Wood went off a 9-2 chance after Fact To File was withdrawn and was bidding to go one better than his second-placed finish behind that horse last year.

The eight-year-old always looked comfortable and drew level with JPR One, who made the running, at the turn for home before pulling clear up the hill to register a 10-length victory over the 2-1 favourite Jonbon.

Image: Heart Wood ridden by Darragh O'Keeffe on their way to winning the Ryanair Chase on day three of the 2026 Cheltenham Festival

Wodhooh end Elliott's wait for a winner this week

Gordon Elliott got off the mark at the Cheltenham Festival when Wodhooh justified favouritism with a game display in the Close Brothers Mares' Hurdle.

The six-year-old won the last race of the meeting, the Martin Pipe Conditional Jockeys' Handicap Hurdle, 12 months ago and proved a welcome tonic for her handler once again after some disappointing reversals in the opening two days of the meeting.

Sent off the 5/6 market leader in the hands of Jack Kennedy after two impressive victories this term, she demonstrated her class to hold off last year's runner-up Jade de Grugy up the run-in and strike at the highest level for the first time.

Image: Wodhooh ridden Jack Kennedy by wins the Close Brothers Mares' Hurdle on day three of the 2026 Cheltenham Festival

Pauling finds form ahead of Gold Cup test

Meetmebythesea provided Ben Pauling and Ben Jones with the perfect Cheltenham Gold Cup appetiser when storming to victory in the Jack Richards Novices' Limited Handicap Chase.

The King George-winning trainer and jockey will go for gold with The Jukebox Man on Friday, but Meetmebythesea had long been talked of as one of their leading chances of the Festival and delivered in style at odds of 9/1.

As has been the theme of the week, Jones had the six-year-old up with the pace throughout and having entered the home straight locked in battle with runner-up Gold Dancer, a tremendous jump at the last enabled Meetmebythesea to surge four and three-quarter lengths clear.

Image: White Noise ridden by Thomas Bellamy (centre) on their way to winning the Ryanair Mares' Novices' Hurdle

Bellamy bags first Festival winner

Tom Bellamy tasted success at the Festival for the first time as White Noise regained the winning thread in the Ryanair Mares' Novices' Hurdle.

A three-time winner earlier in the season, she was then defeated in Grade Two company at Warwick last month, but roared back to form with a brilliant performance from the front end as a 40/1 chance.

Football.title: Trump says it is not 'appropriate' for Iran to play at World Cup
Football.content:
US President Donald Trump says he doesn't "believe it is appropriate" for Iran to play at this summer's World Cup "for their own life and safety".

Trump met FIFA president Gianni Infantino on Tuesday night and told him Iran are still welcome at the tournament and reiterated that in a post on Truth Social before suggesting it is best they stay away.

"The Iran National Soccer Team is welcome to The World Cup, but I really don't believe it is appropriate that they be there, for their own life and safety," he wrote.

The US military and Israel initiated air strikes on Iran on February 28, sparking retaliatory action from Iran on Israel and Middle East nations housing US bases such as Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates.

Trump's post comes after Iran's sports minister ruled out participating in the tournament when he spoke on Iran's state television on Wednesday.

"Considering that ‌this corrupt regime has assassinated our leader, under no circumstances ⁠can we ​participate in ​the World ​Cup," Ahmad Donyamali said.

At this point no official decision has been made on Iran's participation in the competition, which is set to be co-hosted by USA, Canada and Mexico.

Iran's scheduled World Cup fixtures Iran v New Zealand (SoFi Stadium Los Angeles) - June 15

Iran v Belgium (SoFi Stadium Los Angeles) - June 21

Iran v Egypt (Lumen Field Seattle) - June 26

No country has pulled out of the World Cup since 1950 and after speaking with Trump, Infantino said: "This evening, I met with the President of the United States, Donald J. Trump to discuss the status of preparations for the upcoming World Cup, and the growing excitement as we are set to kick off in just 93 days.

"We also spoke about the current situation in Iran, and the fact that the Iranian team has qualified to participate in the World Cup. During the discussions, President Trump reiterated that the Iranian team is, of course, welcome to compete in the tournament in the United States.

"We all need an event like the World Cup to bring people together now more than ever, and I sincerely thank the President of the United States for his support, as it shows once again that football unites the world."

If Iran and USA both finish second in their respective groups, they would face each other in the knockout round. Iran famously beat USA 2-1 at the 1998 World Cup in France.

Iran are due to play their Group G games - against Belgium, Egypt and New Zealand - on the West Coast, which is home to about a million Iranian Americans.

They have played in the past three World Cups and are currently the second highest-ranked Asian team in FIFA rankings (Japan 19, Iran 20).

Who would take Iran's spot?

FIFA's rules state that it could replace Iran or just have three teams in Group G instead of four.

If Iran are replaced, their place would probably be taken by another country from the Asian Football Confederation. Iraq and the United Arab Emirates would have the strongest claims.

Iraq beat UAE in a play off and are now due to play Bolivia or Suriname in an intercontinental play off at the end of this month. Iraq are currently facing serious problems travelling to Mexico for that winner-takes-all fixture.

Football.title: Can Draper keep his Indian Wells title defence going after Djokovic scalp?
Football.content:
Jack Draper said he was feeling “incredible” after his win over Novak Djokovic at Indian Wells, but can he take this form into his next clash with Daniil Medvedev?

Draper, the defending champion, rallied from a set down to beat five-time champ Djokovic in the fourth round on Wednesday..

The 24-year-old, who is coming back after missing eight months due to an arm injury, advanced to the quarter-finals and will face Medvedev, who beat Alex Michelsen 6-2 6-4 in the round of 16.

The in-form Medvedev needed just one hour and 27 minutes to overcome his American opponent, and is flying high having won last month's Dubai Open.

Draper: I'm not playing anywhere near the way I want to play

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Highlights of the Indian Wells match between Novak Djokovic and Jack Draper

Despite his first ever win over Djokovic, Draper admitted he was not feeling at his best ahead of the next round on Thursday, live on Sky Sports Tennis, at midnight.

"Just an incredible feeling," said Draper, who took two hours and 35 minutes to beat the Serbian 4-6 6-4 7-6 (7-5) in one of the best wins of his career.

"I'm out here against Novak, to me the greatest tennis player there is, and someone I've been admiring and watching since I was a little kid.

"So to do that, I was just incredibly proud of myself. It gives me so much confidence. I still don't feel like I'm playing anywhere near the way I want to play.

"I came out here tonight and I won that match through determination and trying to problem solve and do my best and have a great attitude."

Medvedev: When I'm confident, I'm aggressive

Image: Medvedev is in fine form going into the clash with Draper. Pic: Reuters

Draper and Medvedev have only played each other once before, when the Russian won in straight sets in the second round at the ATP Masters 1000 Rome.

Medvedev was aggressive in his win over Michelson, finishing with a 9-1 edge in aces. Following the game, he promised to take that aggression into the contest with Draper.

He said: "When I'm in confidence, I'm an aggressive player.

"So when I'm hitting the ball the way I hit now, I can be aggressive and can put a lot of pressure on my opponents and still be able to be great in defence, and that's what makes it tough."

Henman praises 'unbelievable' Draper display

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Tim Henman acted as a cheerleader during Draper's win over Novak Djokovic, but what did the Sky Sports pundit say to the British No 1?

Former British No 1 Tim Henman heralded Draper's display: "Unbelievable performance - a real rollercoaster. The first two sets, they each had their moments and the early part of the third was absolutely vital. Draper took the match by the scruff of the neck. He got the break. Djokovic looked like he was cooked. He was absolutely exhausted out there.

"Jack had the opportunity at 5-4, to serve the match, and by his own admission, said he completely choked, and we've all been there, but the way he was able to regroup and get that tie-break was a phenomenal effort.

"It's so good for him mentally, physically, emotionally to come through against Djokovic which is why you put the hard yards in. He showed at the end of the match when he got a little bit nervous that he hasn't played many matches. This was only his sixth so to get through that rollercoaster and come out on top, it was really a great night for Jack Draper."

Draper's fellow countryman, Cam Norrie, will take on world No 1 Carlos Alcaraz after beating Australia's Rinky Hijikata 6-4 6-2.

The Spaniard had beaten Norway's Casper Ruud in straight sets.

In the other quarter-final games, Jannik Sinner will face Learner Tien and Arthur Fils takes on Alexander Zverev.

Watch the ATP and WTA Tours, live on Sky Sports or stream with NOW and the Sky Sports app, giving Sky Sports customers access to over 50 per cent more live sport this year at no extra cost. Find out more here.

Football.title: Can Harry Redknapp lift the Gold Cup? Mike Cattermole's big-race runner guide
Football.content:
Who wins the Gold Cup? Sky Sports Racing's Mike Cattermole runs through the 11 runners set to compete in Friday's feature at the Cheltenham Festival.

I am so looking forward to this year's Boodles Gold Cup (4.00), which should be run at a true gallop. You need to stay and have plenty of ability to win Friday's feature, and some will fall short on both counts.

I could certainly see Haiti Couleurs trying to do a Native River by making most. Can anything catch him up the hill? Can Gaelic Warrior last home? Will the real Inothewayurthinkin show up? Is Grey Dawning good enough? Can one of Jango Baie or The Jukebox Man take a big stride forward? Or will there be a shock?

1. Envoi Allen

Jockey: Darragh O'Keeffe | Trainer: Henry de Bromhead

This could be the curtain call for this extraordinary horse who appears at the Cheltenham Festival for the seventh time. He is a three-time Cheltenham Festival winner, landing the Champion Bumper in 2019, the Ballymore Novices Hurdle the following year and then the Ryanair Chase in 2023, the same race he has been placed in for the last two runnings. He was also third in the 2022 Queen Mother Champion Chase.

Sure, he may be past his best, but he was still able to claim his 10th Grade 1 in the Down Royal Champion Chase over three miles last November in great style and was then put by to come into this as a fresh horse. Now he tackles the big one and a new trip for the first time. It is likely to be beyond him but let's hope he comes back unscathed and is set for a long retirement.

Will he be regarded as an all-time great? On ratings, perhaps not, but certainly one of great distinction who promised probably too much when he began his career with 12 straight wins. But who would not always admire and celebrate a 10-time Grade 1 winner?

Image: Envoi Allen and Darragh O'Keeffe win the Grade 1 Betvictor Steeplechase

2. Firefox

Jack Kennedy | Gordon Elliott

This is his third Cheltenham Festival appearance after a third in the 2024 Supreme Novices' Hurdle and a sixth in the Jack Richards last year. He began the season with a Grade 2 win at Down Royal over just short of two and a half miles, confirming that he is a smart chaser.

But this step up in trip is going to stretch him, judged on his fourth in the Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown when he tried three miles for the first time. He was dropped right out and tried to stay on but even those extreme tactics had him treading water at the end, as he finished a neck behind a below-par Galopin Des Champs and 14 lengths behind Fact To File.

Looks overfaced in many ways here.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Anthony Ennis previews the Gold Cup at Cheltenham and shares who he thinks might be worth backing.

3. Gaelic Warrior

Paul Townend | Willie Mullins

A very well-named and extremely talented five-time Grade 1 winner who has shown amazing versatility, trip-wise, in his career. He was an impressive winner of the 3m Grade 1 Novice Hurdle at Punchestown in 2023 and came out the following spring to drop back and win the 2m Arkle Chase here, going one better than in his two previous appearances at the Cheltenham Festival.

Hard to find fault in his last five runs, beating Grey Dawning by 3 lengths in the Aintree Bowl over 3m1f last April, taking the Oaksey Chase with ease at Sandown before reappearing with an epic defeat of stablemate Fact To File in that never-to-be-forgotten John Durkan at Punchestown.

Then he was right in the thick of it in a King George for the ages, beaten two noses by The Jukebox Man and Banbridge, with Jango Baie in fourth. He was no match for Fact To File in the Irish Gold Cup at Leopardstown last month but never gave up, albeit looking all out on the run-in.

His tendency to race a bit keenly, although not always, is a factor to consider as he tries the Gold Cup trip for the first time. At the back of the mind, too, is that he is Flat-bred (by the Niarchos family). But he is trained and ridden by two of the very best. I wish him well but have a feeling he may come up a touch short.

Image: Gaelic Warrior carries the hopes of Willie Mullins this year

4. Gold Tweet

Clement Lefebvre | Gabriel Leenders

A rare French challenger in our most prestigious chase, he won the Grade 2 Cleeve Hurdle here in 2023 and wasn't disgraced when fourth behind Grey Dawning, beaten 16 lengths, last November.

On that evidence, he has much to do here, though was a very creditable third in the Prix la Haye Jousselin (French equivalent of the King George) over just short of three and a half miles last November.

The nine-year-old warmed up with an indifferent run over hurdles at Fontainebleau last month.

5. Grey Dawning

Harry Skelton | Dan Skelton

A strong traveller in his races, he put up a career-best to take the Betfair Chase at Haydock last November from course specialist Royal Pagaille, reversing form from the previous year.

He by-passed the King George and was kept by for the Cotswold Chase here in January when a mistake at the second last proved costly as he took third, beaten ¾-length and 4¼ lengths, behind Spillane's Tower and L'Homme Presse, the winner receiving 6lb from the two placed behind him. It wasn't a bad effort, and Dan Skelton was happy enough in the circumstances.

The trainer will not entertain any thought of his grey failing to stay the trip and so perhaps more of a question is his level of talent. As very smart as he is, is he quite good enough? He was beaten three lengths by Gaelic Warrior in the Aintree Bowl last April, remember.

A previous Cheltenham Festival winner, he won the Turners Novices' Chase here two years ago. On the plus side, he arrives here fresh and will be tuned to the minute.

Image: Grey Dawning leads the Bowl Chase field with Gaelic Warrior (pink cap) stalking in behind

6. Haiti Couleurs

Sean Bowen | Rebecca Curtis

Winner of six of his eight chase starts, he arrives here as an Irish and Welsh National winner, unbeaten in two at Cheltenham, including last year's National Hunt Novice Chase, and with the ability to handle any form of ground. Clearly the stamina box is ticked, in bold, and his style of racing is usually uncomplicated with a sound jumping technique. Expect Sean Bowen to go forward on him to make use of those stamina reserves.

His season has gone well, for the most part very well, excepting the blowout in the Betfair Chase when pulled up after some moderate jumping - most unlike him. Rebecca Curtis assured everybody he would show that to be all wrong, and he duly did the job in style on unusually fast ground in the Welsh National the following month.

Warmed up by dismissing L'Homme Presse in the Denman Chase at Newbury in a professional manner and, although that form needs improving on, there is a chance that there is more to come. Looks sure to run well and a solid each-way shout.

Please use Chrome browser for a more accessible video player Rebecca Curtis spoke to Sky Sports Racing ahead of Haiti Couleurs' date with destiny at the Cheltenham Gold Cup.

7. Inothewayurthinkin

Mark Walsh | Gavin Cromwell

The defending champion who has been a shadow of the horse who ended Galopin Des Champ's reign 12 months ago and yet has been well supported in the market of late to bounce back in his first time cheekpieces.
